title: Employment discrimination based on sexual orientation or gender identity
subtitle: Includes discrimination in hiring, promotion, termination and harassment at work. Only legal status considered, not enforcement.
caption:
Employment discrimination based on sexual orientation or gender identity

Includes discrimination in hiring, promotion, termination and harassment at work. Only legal status considered, not enforcement.

The 2025 world map displays the legal status of employment discrimination protections based on sexual orientation or gender identity across various countries. A significant portion of the globe, including the United States, Canada, Brazil, Australia, and most of Western Europe, is colored blue, indicating legal protections for both sexual orientation and gender identity. In contrast, a large contiguous area spanning Russia, China, the Middle East, and most of Africa is shaded red, signifying no legal protections. Green areas, representing protections for gender identity only, appear in countries such as India, Argentina, and parts of Southern Africa. Light yellow regions, denoting protections for sexual orientation only, are visible in nations like Mexico, South Africa, and several countries in Eastern Europe. A smaller number of countries, including Japan and parts of Southeast Asia, are colored orange, indicating that protections vary by region within the country. Several nations in Central Africa and the Middle East appear in the lightest band or hatched, suggesting no data is available for these locations. The distribution shows a clear geographic clustering, with comprehensive protections concentrated in the Americas and Western Europe, while the absence of protections dominates across Asia and Africa.
